hypothesis: generic-multi-agent baseline (PG 1.0810 SOTA stack, 10× generic)
Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessians (top-100 tokens get 2× weight) plus halved damping (0.01→0.005) to reduce quantization error on common tokens that dominate BPB.

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: top-100 most frequent tokens receive sqrt(2) activation scaling (= 2x weight in H=X^TX), biasing GPTQ to minimize quantization error on common tokens that dominate BPB.
FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian toward top-100 most-frequent tokens (sqrt(2) activation weight → 2× Hessian contribution) so quantization error is minimized on positions covering ~53% of text

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: top-100 most frequent tokens get sqrt(2) activation boost so H = X^T X is 2x heavier on high-frequency positions, biasing int6 quantization to minimize error on tokens that dominate validation BPB.

FreqGPTQ: frequency-weighted Hessian calibration (top-100 tokens get sqrt(2) activation boost → 2x Hessian weight) + lower GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005 to reduce quantization bias on high-frequency tokens and improve overall quant fidelity.

Add SNOO (Step-K Nesterov Outer Optimizer, arXiv:2510.15830): a Lookahead-with-Nesterov wrapper around Muon+Adam that applies outer Nesterov momentum every K=20 inner steps using the pseudo-gradient (fast−slow parameter displacement), then resets fast weights to slow, amplifying persistent optimization directions for better convergence within the fixed 600s budget.

Replace fixed Muon Newton-Schulz coefficients with Polar Express 5-step per-iteration minimax-optimal tuples (arxiv 2505.16932) and reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.01 to 0.005 for better quantization fidelity.
FreqGPTQ: top-100 tokens get 2× Hessian weight via single pilot batch (no pre-collection overhead) + halved GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005), improving quantization fidelity for common tokens.
Z-loss training regularization: add 1e-4 * mean(logsumexp(logits)^2) to cross-entropy in forward(), stabilizing logit magnitudes during training with zero eval overhead to avoid budget overrun.

Combine freq-weighted GPTQ (sqrt2 boost top-100 tokens, CPU-stored batches to cut GPU pressure), GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, and split Muon WD (attn=0.095 vs MLP=0.115) to improve both quantization fidelity and training regularization.
Polar Express NS coefficients + GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, with calibration batches reduced 64→56 to recover the 4s eval overrun seen in exp_009.

SNOO outer Nesterov step disabled during warmdown phase (frac >= 0.28): prevents stale momentum buffer from interfering with LR-decay fine-tuning; SNOO only active during stable training phase (~28% of budget, ~168s).

FreqGPTQ (top-100 tokens 2x Hessian weight) + GPTQ damping 0.005 + TTT freeze first 2 blocks to reduce overfitting and save ~15% TTT backward time; GPU-resident calibration batches to avoid prior eval overrun.
Replace TTT optimizer from SGD (lr=0.005, momentum=0.9) with AdamW (lr=0.001, betas=(0.9,0.999)) — adaptive per-parameter LR should converge more efficiently within the 3-epoch per-chunk window

Z-loss training regularization (1e-4 * logsumexp^2) plus gptq_calibration_batches reduced 64→48 to create ~10s eval headroom against environment jitter (prior run overran by only 3.8s with no eval-side changes).
Polar Express per-iteration NS coefficients for Muon + ttt_epochs 3→2 to clear the systemic eval_budget_overrun (all 18 prior trials DQ'd; TTT dominates eval at ~400s).
Add GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2), to further improve quantization fidelity with zero eval time overhead.

Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2); lower damping reduces quantization bias without adding any eval-time overhead, and the ~130s eval headroom from ttt_epochs=2 keeps us safely under budget.
Set min_lr=0.10 (from 0.0) to floor the warmdown LR at 10% of max, preventing over-annealing in the final ~43s of training; zero eval overhead, mechanism from PR #1787.

AdaMuon: add element-wise second-moment scaling of orthogonalized Muon gradients (beta2=0.95, norm-preserving rescale) to improve update direction quality; muon_beta2 was already a placeholder in hyperparameters, activating it makes Muon direction-adaptive like Adam without changing the magnitude scale.

Z-loss training regularization (1e-4 * mean(logsumexp(logits)^2)) on top of exp_037 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2 + GPTQ damping 0.005): stabilizes logit magnitudes during fast 10-min training; negligible TTT overhead (~0.15s) within the ~10s headroom from the clean baseline.

Replace TTT SGD optimizer with Muon (for 2D matrix params, NS whitening) + SGD (for scalars), ttt_muon_lr=0.001; using the same optimizer as training gives more consistent gradient conditioning during per-chunk adaptation.

Set min_lr=0.10 to floor the LR at 10% of peak during warmdown, preventing the learning rate from collapsing to near-zero in the final ~84s of training (frac≥0.86 with warmdown_frac=0.72), keeping late updates meaningful.

Reset TTT SGD optimizer momentum state between chunks so each chunk adapts independently from clean gradients, preventing stale high-LR early-chunk momentum from contaminating adaptation at later FineWeb document positions.

Increase rope_dims 16→32 (50% of head dims get positional encoding vs 25%) to improve sequence modeling quality, plus reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→48 to save ~8s eval time and reliably clear the ~4.6s environment-jitter eval margin seen in exp_039.

Skip 'quantized' eval step (removes one full torch.compile(eval_model) call, ~165s compilation overhead) when TTT is enabled, while keeping z-loss training regularization; reduces eval from ~605s to ~440s in this environment.

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine decay (0.5*(1+cos(π*progress))) over the same warmdown_frac=0.72; cosine keeps LR ~14% higher than linear at the 25% warmdown mark, giving more effective gradient steps for this severely undertrained model while converging to zero at the same endpoint.

Add nesterov=True to TTT SGD optimizer: zero computational overhead vs classical momentum, faster convergence via lookahead gradient (proven in convex settings), pure eval-path change so model weights and GPTQ timing match exp_037.

TTT per-chunk SGD momentum reset (optimizer.state.clear) + reduce GPTQ calibration batches 64→48 to gain ~3s eval headroom; momentum reset prevents cross-document momentum contamination across FineWeb chunks.

Revert rope_dims back to 16 (exp_058 showed 32 hurts +0.003), reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→32 to save ~16s eval time and reliably clear the 4.4s environment-jitter budget margin; tests whether 32 calibration batches preserves GPTQ quality at exp_037 level.

GPTQ quality: (1) separate MLP clip sigma 14.0 vs attn 12.85 — PR #1769 shows wider MLP sigma preserves 4× hidden-dim outliers; (2) block_size 128→32 — finer GPTQ error-propagation reduces lazy-inversion approximation error; both changes are eval-side only with zero train overhead.
Set min_lr=0.1 on top of exp_037 (PE NS + ttt_epochs=2 + GPTQ damping=0.005): floors LR at 10% of max during last ~7% of training, preventing late-training LR collapse.

Reduce warmdown_frac 0.72→0.60 to extend peak-LR phase from 168s to 240s: with warmdown_frac=0.72 the LR warmdown starts at 168s but depth recurrence only activates at 210s (enable_looping_at=0.35), so the recurrence currently trains entirely during warmdown and never at peak LR; with 0.60 the peak-LR window extends to 240s, giving recurrence 30s at peak LR and all layers 43% more aggressive-update time before the decay.

Skip pre-quantization diagnostic eval (removes one torch._dynamo.reset()+recompile of FP32 model, saving ~90-136s) and restore ttt_epochs 2→3 (adds ~29s for better adaptation); net eval ~497-543s clears environment-jitter budget while recovering TTT quality sacrificed in exp_023.

Add z-loss regularization (weight 1e-4): penalizes log-partition function (logsumexp(logits)²) during training to prevent logit diffusion and encourage confident predictions; complements logit_softcap by targeting the full softmax denominator; primary metric (quantized_ttt) uses forward_logits for scoring so is unaffected by z-loss in forward.

Replace MLP LeakyReLU² with SwiGLU (iso-param: hidden=2/3×full_hidden=1344, fc: 512→2688, proj: 1344→512) — learned gating consistently outperforms fixed activations for LM training; pure architecture change with near-zero eval overhead.

Skip redundant quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (~60s saved) + keep GPTQ improvements (mlp_clip_sigmas=14.0, block_size=32); sliding_window is a fallback metric superseded by quantized_ttt, so skipping it is lossless for the leaderboard metric.

SWA: average 5 EMA snapshots taken at frac=0.75–0.95 of training (late warmdown) to reduce model variance and improve final checkpoint quality, plus eval timing fix: reuse eval_model for TTT (skip second deserialize+dynamo.reset) saving ~68s of recompilation overhead.

Keep pre-quant eval skip (confirmed saves ~94s, eval=510s), restore ttt_epochs=2 (3 epochs confirmed worse), and add min_lr=0.1 to floor warmdown LR at 10% of peak; PR #1787 mechanism: prevents LR from collapsing to near-zero in final ~84s of training, keeping gradients meaningful.
Replace standard mean CE loss with MiLe Loss (entropy-weighted CE): weight=(1−H(p)/log(V)), down-weighting inherently ambiguous positions while focusing gradient on learnable clear-pattern tokens.

Reuse eval_model for TTT (skip second deserialize + torch._dynamo.reset) to eliminate one torch.compile call (~68s saved from cache-hit on eval_model.forward_logits compiled by eval_val_sliding), plus ttt_lr 0.005→0.007 for stronger per-chunk adaptation.

FreqGPTQ (top-100 token √2 Hessian boost) + ttt_epochs 2→1 + ttt_lr 0.005→0.010: FreqGPTQ biases GPTQ calibration toward high-frequency tokens for better quantization fidelity where BPB cost is highest; ttt_epochs reduction saves ~30s eval time to reliably clear the budget on slow environments.

Split Muon weight decay for MLP vs attention matrices: attn matrices keep muon_wd=0.095, MLP matrices get muon_wd_mlp=0.115 (higher WD tightens MLP weight norms which have 4x wider hidden dim); the seed description mentions this split but it was lost in the exp_023→037 chain; confirmed useful in the prior 500-trial run (exp_164 kept).

ttt_epochs 2→1 to reliably clear slow-environment eval budget (TTT alone ~570s with 2 epochs → ~285s with 1), plus GPTQ quality improvements (mlp_clip_sigmas=14.0, block_size=32) and skip redundant sliding_window eval; 3→2 epochs improved quality in exp_023, probing whether 2→1 continues that trend.

FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian calibration toward top-100 most frequent tokens by applying sqrt(2)x activation scaling per position before H=X^TX accumulation, so quantization optimizes error preferentially on the ~53% of text dominated by frequent tokens that have highest BPB impact.

FreqGPTQ (top-100 token 2x Hessian boost) + remove pre-quantization post-ema timed_eval to save ~98s eval time (68s recompile + 30s eval), clearing the budget while keeping ttt_epochs=2 and obtaining a valid BPB measurement for FreqGPTQ quality.

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian (top-100 frequent tokens get 2x Hessian weight via sqrt(2)x activation scaling) + skip pre-quantization diagnostic eval (~90-130s saved): FreqGPTQ biases calibration toward preserving accuracy for common tokens that dominate BPB, while removing the pre-quant eval clears the eval budget overrun that has blocked all exp_037 children.

Remove torch._dynamo.reset() before TTT (dynamo cache from eval_val_sliding reused by eval_val_ttt, saving ~68s recompile) + split Muon WD: MLP matrices get muon_wd_mlp=0.115 vs attention/other 0.095, matching seed stack intended design.

Cautious Weight Decay (CWD) for Muon: apply WD only when parameter and NS-whitened gradient have the same sign (p·g>0), so WD and gradient update are directionally aligned. Drop-in change per arXiv 2510.12402 — consistently improves LM pre-training loss, requires no new hyperparameters, touches only the training step.

Skip pre-quant post-ema eval (~121s), remove both torch._dynamo.reset() calls to preserve forward_logits compile cache pre-warmed during warmup, and reduce GPTQ damping 0.005→0.003 following exp_037's trend.

Token-frequency-stratified training loss: compute inverse-frequency weights (freq^-0.3) from 8 training batches, upweighting rare tokens 2-4× to direct more gradient signal toward harder-to-predict tokens; training-only change (eval/TTT model uses standard CE via non-persistent buffer defaulting to ones after deserialize).

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): pass first-layer V to all subsequent attention layers and blend as v=(v_n+v_1)*0.5, alleviating attention concentration; no new parameters, no eval overhead.

Skip the pre-quantization FP32 eval while keeping torch._dynamo.reset(), saving ~99s of eval time without changing val_bpb — exp_072's regression was from ttt_epochs=3 (not the skip), so this single-line removal safely brings eval_s from 580s to ~480s, creating 120s headroom for all future experiments.

Batch TTT gradient AllReduce: replace ~120 individual dist.all_reduce(p.grad) calls per backward step with a single batched AllReduce of all gradients flattened into one tensor, reducing NCCL per-call overhead by ~120x per step. With ~36K backward passes across 2 TTT epochs, each step saving ~1-10ms of NCCL overhead = 36-360s total eval savings, reliably clearing the eval budget that all exp_037 children have hit.

Muon-VS: add per-element RMS tracking of the Nesterov gradient (float32, beta2=0.95) and scale by 1/sqrt(rms_hat) BEFORE Newton-Schulz orthogonalization, using the already-defined but unused muon_beta2 hyperparameter; ordering before NS is critical per arXiv 2601.14603 which shows 1.36x convergence speedup vs standard Muon.

Remove pre-quant diagnostic eval (~121s savings) and pre-TTT dynamo.reset() (potential ~90s cache-hit savings), plus reduce GPTQ damping 0.005→0.003 for slightly better quantization fidelity, to fix eval_budget_overrun for all exp_037 children.

Skip both torch._dynamo.reset() and pre-quant FP32 eval (lines 1537-1538), keeping ttt_epochs=2 — triton kernel cache from training compile warms subsequent evals, so skipping the first dynamo.reset lets later torch.compile calls be fast cache-hits instead of cold ~70s each; exp_072 confirmed 510s eval with both skipped but had ttt_epochs=3 causing regression; exp_096 showed keeping dynamo.reset kills the savings.
Remove first torch._dynamo.reset()+pre-quant eval (saves ~120s, matching exp_072's valid-run pattern) and set min_lr=0.1 (floors warmdown LR at 10% of peak in final ~42s); ttt_epochs=2 unchanged.

Skip redundant quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled: the sliding_window (~60s) is superseded by the quantized_ttt metric, so skipping it when ttt_enabled saves ~60s with zero quality impact and clears the 4.5s eval overrun seen in exp_097.

Skip redundant quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (~60s saved, primary metric is quantized_ttt anyway) + add gentle linear position-weighted CE 0.9→1.1 across sequence positions to GPT.forward (higher weight on late positions with more context, consistent with TTT adaptation; kept as exp_156 in prior 500-trial run per LESSONS.md).

Full-training QAT (STE) in CastedLinear: simulate int6 per-row SDClip quantization during every training forward pass (clip_sigmas=12.85, clip_range=31), forcing weights to cluster near quantization grid so GPTQ has less error to correct; also remove pre-quant diagnostic eval to save ~30s eval margin.

Reuse eval_model for TTT (remove second dynamo.reset()+deserialize), saving ~1 torch.compile call (~90s) via cache hit on forward_logits; add RoPE cache clearing in eval_val_ttt to fix inference-tensor crash from exp_081.

TTT partial-layer adaptation: freeze all blocks except the last ttt_adapt_layers=4 (blocks 7-10) during TTT backward passes, reducing backward compute by ~50% (backward terminates at block 6 boundary), enabling ttt_epochs=3 within ~440s eval vs ~595s full-model epochs=2 — 3 epochs of focused output-layer adaptation vs 2 epochs of full-model adaptation.

Async data prefetch: background thread pre-builds next CPU batch while GPU computes current batch, hiding mmap/numpy data-preparation latency (~20-30ms/step) and yielding ~500-1000 extra training steps in the same 600s wall clock with zero eval overhead.

Partial-TTT: freeze blocks 0-6 during TTT adaptation, only adapt last 4 blocks (7-10) + skip_weights + skip_gates + final_norm. Reduces backward from ~17 block-passes to ~4 (~76% cut), dropping per-epoch TTT cost from ~150s to ~35s — enabling 2 full TTT epochs in ~380s total eval vs ~580s currently.

Set min_lr=0.1 to floor LR warmdown at 10% of peak: prevents near-zero learning rates in the final ~7% of training (frac=0.93–1.0), keeping late-warmdown updates meaningful. Tried 3x on exp_037 children but always hit eval_budget_overrun; exp_102 confirmed 137s eval headroom, making this finally testable.

Skip redundant quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (~60s saved) + gentle position-weighted CE 0.9→1.1 via pre-registered model buffer _pos_weight (avoids torch.linspace inside compiled graph, fixes exp_103 crash); buffer is persistent=False so reinitialised on deserialize, applies equally to TTT adaptation loss for training/TTT consistency.
Skip pre-quant eval, move torch._dynamo.reset() to after serialize (prevents training-graph crash seen in exp_101) + min_lr=0.1 quality improvement.
One-deserialize + RoPE cache clearing (from exp_105) + reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→48 to save ~8s and clear the 4.5s overrun from exp_105 (eval_s=604.5s).

ttt_epochs=1 (saves ~111s, reliably clears the systemic eval_budget_overrun) + LAWA weight averaging replaces EMA: take 36 uniform snapshots every 2% of training during warmdown (frac 0.28–1.0) and average them uniformly, improving generalization vs EMA which only captures the last ~30s of training.

Explicitly pass block_size=32 to gptq_quantize_weight (seed description specifies block_size=32 but function defaulted to 128), giving 4× finer GPTQ column-block Hessian-guided error propagation for marginally better int6 quantization quality at zero eval-time and artifact-size cost.

Skip sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (saves ~60s, clears eval budget) + remove TTT cosine LR decay → constant h.ttt_lr throughout all chunks, preventing near-zero adaptation in later validation chunks and maximizing total TTT quality.

FreqGPTQ (top-100 frequent tokens get sqrt(2)x Hessian weight via activation scaling in calibration hooks) + skip quantized_sliding_window when TTT enabled (~60s savings clears eval budget, proven by exp_102); FreqGPTQ biases GPTQ to minimize quantization error on the tokens that dominate BPB.

Partial-TTT v2: freeze ALL params including skip_weights/gates for TTT, only unfreeze last 4 blocks (7-10) + final_norm. Skip_weights trainable in v1 caused requires_grad=True to propagate from the first decoder pass, negating savings; v2 ensures all frozen-path tensors have requires_grad=False so autograd genuinely stops at block[7] input, cutting backward ~76%.

Skip quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (~60s savings, proven safe in exp_102) + add position-weighted CE (linear ramp 0.9→1.1 over sequence positions) to GPT.forward: upweights later positions where more context is available, focuses gradient where the sliding-window eval actually scores, improving final model quality (prior run exp_156 was kept per exp_103 notes).

Remove redundant compiled_model = torch.compile(eval_model) + timed_eval("quantized") step and second deserialize/dynamo.reset(), reuse single eval_model for TTT with RoPE cache clearing to eliminate ~95s eval overhead and fix eval_budget_overrun.

ttt_epochs=1 (saves ~111s) + skip pre-quant FP32 eval keeping dynamo.reset (saves ~120s) + LAWA uniform weight averaging over the last 8% of training (frac=0.92–1.0, ~8 snapshots at LR<11% peak) replacing EMA, to average over the flat basin at low LR rather than exponentially weighting only the final 29s.
Dynamic batch curriculum: scale train_batch_tokens from 50% (frac<0.15) → 75% (frac<0.45) → 100% to give ~2x more gradient steps in early high-gradient training with zero eval overhead.

Skip redundant "quantized" eval when TTT is enabled (saves ~125s: 1 deserialize + 1 torch.compile + eval_val) + replace cosine-decay TTT LR with constant ttt_lr (uniform adaptation across all chunks; previously second half of chunks got <10% LR wasting TTT budget).

Skip sliding_window eval when TTT enabled (saves ~60s) + halve TTT chunk size 32768→16384 tokens: doubles adaptation ticks (152→304 chunks) at same total compute, letting the model track finer-grained distribution shifts in the validation sequence.

Skip quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (saves ~60s, fixes systemic eval_budget_overrun) + split Muon weight decay: attn matrices keep muon_wd=0.095 while MLP matrices get muon_wd_mlp=0.115 (larger hidden dim needs tighter norm control), matching the seed stack's intended design confirmed useful in the prior 500-trial run.

Skip quantized_sliding_window when TTT enabled (~60s savings, proven by exp_102) + increase GPTQ calibration batches 64→96 (50% more Hessian data → better quantization fidelity); exp_067 showed 32 batches hurt quality, so 96 should improve it, and the 60s window-skip provides budget headroom for the extra ~15s calibration cost.

Skip quantized_sliding_window when TTT is enabled (saves ~60s, confirmed safe in exp_102) + min_lr=0.1 (floors warmdown LR at 10%, improves quality ~0.001 confirmed in exp_070/exp_111): together these clear the systemic eval_budget_overrun while improving quality below current best 1.078936.

Skip quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT enabled (~60s savings, proven in exp_102) + split Muon weight decay: MLP matrices get muon_wd_mlp=0.115 vs non-MLP matrices keep muon_wd=0.095, restoring the intended MuonEq-R design where wider MLP projections (4× hidden dim) receive stronger weight-decay regularization; confirmed kept as exp_164 in prior 500-trial run per exp_083 notes.

Change TTT scoring phase from torch.no_grad to torch.inference_mode to match eval_val_sliding's dynamo guard, achieving a compile cache hit and eliminating a ~90s recompile; add per-chunk RoPE cache clearing before adaptation to prevent inference-tensor autograd failures.

Skip "quantized" eval when TTT enabled (~40s saved) + constant TTT LR (no cosine decay; uniform adaptation) + ttt_epochs 2→1: constant LR distributes same total effective learning (99×ttt_lr) as cosine+2 but uniformly, so 1 epoch constant ≈ 2 epochs cosine in quality while halving TTT time.

Add z-loss auxiliary term (coeff=1e-4): penalize log-partition function magnitude via logsumexp(logits)^2, reducing logit scale variance and activation outliers for better GPTQ quantization quality; pure training-side change, zero eval overhead.
From exp_131 (constant-LR TTT, clean eval path): increase ttt_epochs 1→2 to give 2× gradient signal per chunk while staying well within eval budget (~320s expected vs 600s limit).

Raise ttt_momentum 0.9→0.95 (smoother cross-chunk adaptation memory per PR#995 TTT pattern) + increase gptq_calibration_batches 64→96 (50% more Hessian data for better GPTQ calibration); both on top of exp_131's 467.8s eval baseline giving ~30s overhead headroom.

GPTQ damping 0.005→0.002 (extending exp_037's 0.01→0.005 improvement trend for less quantization bias) + Nesterov SGD for TTT (lookahead gradient via accumulated cross-chunk momentum for better per-chunk adaptation direction) from exp_131 base.

Add min_lr=0.1 on top of exp_131 (ttt_epochs=1 + skip quantized eval + constant TTT LR): floors warmdown LR at 10% of peak, preventing over-annealing in the final ~84s of training; this pure training change has shown ~0.001 quality improvement in exp_070, exp_111, and exp_079 without adding any eval overhead.

Add sparse per-head attention gate (arXiv:2505.06708, Qwen GatedAttn, NeurIPS 2025 Oral) to all 11 blocks: sigmoid gate on attention output conditioned on first 12 residual dims, 2*sigmoid zero-init for transparency, routed to scalar AdamW; introduces input-dependent non-linearity per head to reduce attention sink and improve calibration.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): pass layer-0 raw V tensor to all subsequent layers, blend as v_attn = v + vr_alpha*v0 with learnable vr_alpha zero-init (transparent start). XSA keeps original v for denoising semantics. Should improve information flow through depth-recurrence loops, matching paper's ~20% training efficiency gain.

On top of exp_146 (attention gate + eval savings): add min_lr=0.1 (confirmed +0.000163 from exp_145) + FreqGPTQ (top-100 frequent tokens get sqrt(2)× weight in GPTQ Hessian calibration, biasing quantization toward tokens that dominate validation BPB).

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): compute vr0 = blocks[0].c_v(rms_norm(embedding)) once per forward, blend into all blocks 1-10 as v = v + vr_alpha*vr0 with learnable scalar per block (zero-init, transparent at start). ResFormer showed 16% parameter-efficiency gain; here vr_alpha learns to anchor later-layer attention to the original embedding values, complementing the existing attention gate.

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): vr0=blocks[0].c_v(rms_norm(embedding)) blended into V of blocks 1-10 via learnable vr_alpha (zero-init, transparent). Fix: replaced 'has_vr and v0 is not None' guard with plain 'has_vr' to avoid fullgraph=True graph-break from Python identity check on v0.

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): use F.linear(rms_norm(x0), c_v0_weight) instead of c_v module.forward() to avoid fullgraph=True module-called-twice compilation failure; vr_alpha per block 1-10 (zero-init) blends vr0 into each layer's V.

Add scalar input-conditioned MLP output gate to all 11 blocks (complement to per-head attn gate from exp_146): 2*sigmoid(x_in[:12]@mlp_gate_w + mlp_gate_b) applied to MLP output; zero-init → transparent at step 0, learns to suppress/amplify MLP contribution per position; 143 new params, zero eval overhead.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): compute v0=blocks[0].attn.c_v(blocks[0].attn_norm(x0)) once before the block loop, blend into all 11 blocks' value vectors as v += vr_alpha*v0 (zero-init alpha, no conditional → fullgraph-safe). +11 scalar params, +82 compressed code bytes.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): compute v0=blocks[0].attn.c_v(x0) once from normalized embedding, add vr_alpha[num_kv_heads]*v0 to each block's V (zero-init, fully fullgraph-safe — always a tensor, no None checks). Expected ~-0.001 from exp_160 signal.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): reuse blocks[0].attn.c_v to compute v0=c_v(x0) once before the block loop, blend into each block's V as v += vr_alpha*v0 (zero-init scalar per block). No new weight matrices — 11 new float16 scalars (22 bytes). Fixes size_blocked in exp_160/171/172/173 which all added separate CastedLinear projections.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897) fixed alpha=0.5 on top of exp_169: v_attn=v+0.5*v0 where v0 is block-0's raw V tensor (zeros for block-0 itself). Zero new parameters — only +110 code bytes, stays within 16MB budget.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): v0=blocks[0].attn.c_v(blocks[0].attn_norm(x0)) computed once, blended into all 11 blocks' value vectors as v += vr_alpha.to(dtype=x.dtype)*v0 (zero-init alpha, dtype-safe, no conditional → fullgraph-compatible).

Cosine warmdown for LR schedule: replace linear decay with cosine (matching existing Muon-momentum cosine warmdown), keeping LR higher early in warmdown and decaying more steeply at the end — zero parameter change, pure schedule improvement.

ResFormer value residual via linear trick: vr_alpha per attn block blends rms_norm(embedding) into V using c_v(x + vr_alpha*x0) single-call — zero-init transparent, learns direct embedding path to V. +45 code bytes vs exp_169.

ResFormer value residual v2: use embedding slice x0[...,:kv_dim].reshape(B,T,kv_h,hd) as v0 instead of c_v(x0) — avoids double GPTQ-hook firing that inflated artifact by 13KB in exp_184; blends into each block's V via per-block vr_alpha scalar (zero-init, 11 new float16 scalars = 22 bytes total).

ResFormer value residual: single vr_alpha[num_layers] in GPT (not per-block), pre-scale v0b per block index before passing — eliminates 10x tensor-metadata overhead vs exp_187, saves ~1500 bytes in artifact. Fixes exp_187 size_blocked issue.

Input-conditioned skip gate on each U-Net skip connection (analogous to attention gate exp_146 and MLP gate exp_169): 2*sigmoid(x[:12]@skip_input_gate_w[i] + skip_input_gate_b[i]) multiplicatively scales each scaled_skip before the lerp; zero-init → transparent at step 0, learns per-position amplification/suppression. Reverts failed exp_185 value-residual. 104 new scalar params (8 skips × 13), AdamW via CONTROL_TENSOR_NAME_PATTERNS.

Embedding shortcut to attention output: add vr_alpha*rms_norm(embedding) to proj(y) in each attention block — direct path from original embedding to residual stream without changing c_v inputs, preserving model compression behavior.

Replace final EMA model with LAWA (Latest Weight Averaging): collect up to 12 snapshots in the last 10% of training (frac≥0.90, every 15 steps), then average uniformly instead of applying EMA. LAWA's uniform average over the late-warmdown window should give a cleaner optimum than EMA's exponential decay, which still includes noisy high-LR checkpoints.

ResFormer value residual: compute v0=blocks[0].attn.c_v(embedding) once before the block loops, blend into all blocks as v += vr_alpha*v0 (zero-init, transparent start). All blocks get vr_alpha (no conditional flag, avoids DDP issues that crashed exp_162). Exp_160 showed 1.074723 (better than best) but was size_blocked; this implementation adds only 76 packed bytes vs exp_156.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): capture V_raw from block 0 inside attention forward, mix into blocks 1-10 as (V_n + V_0)*0.5. Zero new parameters—V_0 is a runtime activation threaded via v0_res argument. Static use_v_residual flag per block ensures torch.compile traces constant branches. exp_160 showed this direction reaches bpb=1.074723 but was size_blocked; this implementation adds no parameters so artifact size is unchanged.

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): vr_alpha added ONLY to blocks 1-10 in GPT.__init__ (not in CausalSelfAttention.__init__), preventing DDP allreduce crash from block-0's unused parameter; vr0=F.linear(rms_norm(x0), c_v0_weight) computed once in forward_logits, blended into V of blocks 1-10.

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): add per-block vr_alpha scalar (zero-init) that blends normalized embedding features into each attention's V computation via v = v + vr_alpha * F.linear(x0_normed, c_v.weight). Uses F.linear to bypass GPTQ hooks (no Hessian contamination). Creates direct embedding→V shortcut, improving information flow through deep layers.

Value residual with detached v0: v0=blocks[0].attn.c_v(x0).reshape(...).detach() — detach stops gradient through v0 computation (c_v.weight/embedding train normally like exp_156, fixing the 14.7KB model size inflation), while vr_alpha still learns via dL/dvr_alpha=dL/dv×v0_values. Exp_172 showed 1.073363 but was size_blocked; detach preserves ~70-80% of the improvement.

Value residual (detach v0) + LOWBIT_LAYERS blocks.10.attn.c_k at int5: fixes exp_182's 606-byte size overrun — c_k int5 saves ~7KB compressed (keys undergo rms_norm so error is diluted, minimal BPB impact ~0.0003), giving ~6.3KB headroom vs 3σ seed variance of ~5.1KB.

FreqGPTQ: weight GPTQ Hessian by token frequency — top-100 most frequent tokens get sqrt(2) activation multiplier (2x Hessian weight), directing quantization error minimization toward the tokens that dominate BPB average.

Add scalar MLP output gate to all 11 blocks: analogous to exp_146's per-head attention gate, gate_w=(1,12) zero-init, gate_b=(1,) zero-init, applied as mlp_out *= 2*sigmoid(F.linear(x[:12], gate_w)+gate_b) after MLP forward, before mlp_scale. Routes via mlp_gate pattern to scalar AdamW (not Muon). Adds 13 params/layer × 11 = 143 total params (~286 bytes float16 in artifact).

ResFormer value residual v2 (fix fullgraph crash): pre-seed v0 as zeros tensor, run block 0 explicitly to capture real v0, then pass to all remaining enc+dec blocks. Avoids None→tensor mutation that broke fullgraph=True. v_attn = v + vr_alpha*v0, zero-init alpha; XSA uses original v.

Replace LeakyReLU² MLP with SwiGLU (hidden=1344 vs 2048, matched params via 2/3× rule): gated activation allows learned feature selection, universally adopted in modern LLMs (LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma) over non-gated variants.

Add min_lr=0.1 on top of exp_146 (sparse attention gate): floors warmdown LR at 10% of peak, combining two proven independent improvements — exp_145 showed +0.000163 at exp_131 level, now stacking with exp_146's attention gate.

Add min_lr=0.1 on top of exp_146 (attention gate baseline): floors warmdown LR at 10% of peak, same change that gave -0.000163 on exp_131 branch (exp_145), now applied to exp_146 branch which adds the attention gate on top.

Add ResFormer-style value residual (arXiv:2410.17897): compute vr0=c_v(attn_norm(x0)) once from initial embedding, blend into each block's V as v += vr_alpha * vr0 with zero-init scalar per block. exp_160 validated this gives val_bpb=1.074723 but was 315B over budget; compact reimplementation should fit.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897) on top of exp_164: pre-compute v0 from block-0's c_v applied to x0, add alpha_i * v0 to v in each block 1-10 (zero-init alpha → transparent start); exp_160 on exp_146 achieved 1.074723 (-0.0014) but was size_blocked, this compact implementation avoids the duplicate-block-0 code bloat.

ResFormer value residual (exp_176 follow-up): add LOWBIT_LAYERS support and default to int5 for blocks.0.attn.c_k and blocks.1.attn.c_k — K is RMSNorm'd so int5 c_k has minimal BPB cost; saves ~5-20 KB in brotli artifact to clear the 3,232-byte overage from exp_176.

Add input-conditioned scalar MLP output gate to all 11 blocks on top of exp_186 (value residual + attn gate): zero-init [1,12] weight + [1] bias per block, conditions on first 12 residual dims, 2*sigmoid → transparent at init; same design as the per-head attn gate (exp_146) but applied to MLP path — exp_169 showed this gate gave −0.001262 on attn-gate baseline, so stacking it on the value-residual+min_lr current best should give ~−0.001 additive gain.

Port two improvements from exp_169 branch onto exp_186 best: (1) scalar input-conditioned MLP output gate (2*sigmoid zero-init, 2 params/block, AdamW-trained) — gave -0.000737 on exp_156→169; (2) FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian calibration toward top-100 frequent tokens via sqrt(2) activation weighting — combining two orthogonal gains from different kept branches onto the current best.

Add scalar input-conditioned MLP output gate (exp_169 design) on top of exp_186 (value residual + int5 c_k): 13 params/block × 11 = 143 params total, zero-init → transparent at step 0, learns to modulate MLP per position.

Add QAT (Quantization-Aware Training) via STE: simulate int6 SDClip quantization in CastedLinear.forward (scale=12.85*row_std/31, STE detach) gated by a non-persistent buffer, enabled at qat_start_frac=0.5 (50% through training). Model aligns weights to the int6 grid before GPTQ, reducing the ~0.019 BPB quantization degradation gap.

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897) + LOWBIT_LAYERS int5 for blocks.10.mlp.proj to fix size: vr0=c_v(attn_norm(x0)) blended into all blocks' V via zero-init vr_alpha; int5 on last MLP proj saves ~10KB to stay under 16MB cap (exp_175 size_blocked at +1546B).

QAT via STE (fixed): _qat_enabled Python bool instance attr on CastedLinear, constant-folded False by torch.compile at eval (zero overhead). Pre-compile QAT variants during warmup (budget-free). At qat_start_frac=0.5 training aligns weights to int6 SDClip grid, reducing quantization gap before GPTQ.

Value residual via x0-slice (no c_v double-call): vr0=x0[...,:kv_dim].view(B,T,Hkv,D) anchors all blocks' V to the first 256 dims of normalized embedding; zero-init vr_alpha per block; avoids the Hessian-inflation that caused exp_175 to be size_blocked.

Add per-head-group MLP output gate (analogous to exp_146's attention gate): 8 groups of 64 channels, conditioned on first 12 dims of residual stream, 2*sigmoid zero-init → transparent; plus min_lr=0.1 to prevent LR collapse in final warmdown phase.

Byte-weighted training loss: weight each token's CE by its UTF-8 byte count (from precomputed LUT), directly aligning training objective with BPB metric — rare multi-byte tokens get proportionally more gradient signal.

ResFormer value residual (arXiv:2410.17897) + gate_dim 12→8 to fix size: v_attn=v+vr_alpha*v0 blends layer-0 V into all subsequent layers. Gate dim reduced 12→8 saves ~1000 bytes compressed (1408 bytes flat) to bring artifact under 16MB limit.
Add min_lr=0.1 to exp_146 (attention gate base): floors warmdown LR at 10% of peak, preventing over-annealing in final ~84s, validated by exp_145 (Δ=-0.000163 on exp_131).
Increase GPTQ calibration batches 64→128: 2x Hessian samples improves quantization accuracy (√2 better estimate), using the ~152s eval headroom revealed by exp_139 (eval_s=447.6s vs 600s budget).

Restore bifurcated q_gain [H,2] (separate scales for RoPE/NoPE dims) and sparse attn-output gate [H,12] (zero-init, 2*sigmoid(x[:12]@gate_w) per head) from the original multi_596 baseline, both removed in the exp_023 lineage; adds per-dim Q scaling expressiveness and learned contextual gating with no eval overhead.
From exp_131: ttt_epochs 1→2 (constant LR, 2× per-chunk gradient signal) + reduce GPTQ calibration batches 64→48 (saves ~12s to clear the 4.3s eval overrun seen in exp_140).

FreqGPTQ: weight top-100 most-frequent tokens with sqrt(2) activation boost during Hessian calibration, giving them 2× weight in H=X^TX so GPTQ optimizes quantization error preferentially for high-frequency tokens that dominate BPB.

FreqGPTQ: pre-collect calibration batches, compute top-100 most-frequent-token map, apply sqrt(2)x activation scaling in Hessian hooks → 2x Hessian weight for frequent tokens, biasing GPTQ to protect high-BPB-impact positions; exp_116 tried this but hit eval overrun (now fixed by exp_131's skip-eval, 132s headroom).
From exp_131: ttt_lr 0.005→0.010, keep ttt_epochs=1 — doubles per-chunk gradient magnitude with zero extra eval cost vs the 2-epoch approach that overran the budget.
LAWA weight averaging: replace EMA with uniform average of ~12 snapshots taken every 2% of budget during the last 25% of training (frac≥0.75), finding flatter loss minima with zero eval overhead.

Skip quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (saves ~60s, proven to fit budget by exp_102) + fix TTT condition to not require sliding_window_enabled + add nesterov=True to TTT SGD optimizer (zero overhead, lookahead gradient for faster per-chunk adaptation).

Skip sliding_window eval when TTT enabled (saves ~60s eval time) + increase GPTQ block_size 128→256 for better inter-column error compensation in the quantization update step, potentially improving quantization quality at the same artifact size.

On top of exp_126 (skip sliding_window + split Muon WD), also skip pre-quantization FP32 eval when TTT is enabled: the pre-quant eval triggers a full recompile of compiled_model (~90s) plus the eval itself (~30s), totaling ~120s wasted since the FP32 BPB is superseded by quantized_ttt anyway; this should bring eval_s from ~604s to ~484s.

min_lr=0.1 + skip pre-quant eval (~120s saved, proven safe in exp_072) + skip sliding_window when TTT enabled (~60s saved): combined ~180s savings reliably clears the 604s overrun from exp_128, while min_lr=0.1 floors the warmdown LR at 10% for quality improvement.

Differential Attention (ICLR 2025): halve num_heads 8→4, double head_dim 64→128 (same projection sizes), split Q/K/V into two 64-dim sub-heads per head and compute y=(A1-λA2)@V using 4 FA3 calls, plus per-head SubLayerNorm; λ initialized via sigmoid(0)=0.5; ttt_epochs 2→1 to compensate for ~20% eval overhead from extra FA3 calls.

Skip quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT enabled (~117s savings per exp_102, eval→462s) + recover split Muon WD: attn=0.095, MLP=0.115 (lost from multi_596 baseline in exp_023 chain; higher WD regularizes the wider 2048-dim MLP matrices).

Skip quantized_sliding_window eval when TTT is enabled (saves ~60s, proven by exp_102 to fit eval budget) + nesterov=True in TTT SGD optimizer (zero overhead, lookahead gradient for faster per-chunk adaptation). TTT gate condition kept identical to exp_037.

Batch TTT gradient AllReduce: flatten all parameter grads into one tensor, single dist.all_reduce, then scatter back — replaces ~200 serial NCCL calls per backward step with 1, saving ~142s of eval time (exp_102 showed batched AllReduce + skip_sliding_window reaches eval_s=462s vs 604s without); combined with skip_sliding_window + split Muon WD (MLP=0.115 vs attn=0.095).

Skip both pre-quant FP32 eval (~99s) AND quantized_sliding_window when TTT (~117s) for ~216s total budget savings, plus Muon WD split (attn=0.095, MLP=0.115) to recover the multi_596 baseline optimizer design lost in the exp_023 chain.

Skip pre-quantization FP32 eval (~100s savings: retrace+eval avoided) + skip quantized_sliding_window when TTT enabled (~60s savings) + nesterov=True in TTT SGD; combined ~160s eval savings makes the pipeline robust to slow-environment jitter that caused exp_141 to overrun at 604.7s.

Remove torch.cuda.empty_cache() before TTT deserialization (saves ~2-3s, 80GB GPU has ample headroom after del eval_model) and reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→48 (saves ~2s GPTQ overhead); together trimming ~5s from the 2.8s overrun seen in exp_143 to clear the budget while keeping the quality stack intact.

FreqGPTQ interleaved (top-100 tokens √2 Hessian boost, freq estimated from first 8 calib batches, zero pre-collection overhead) + GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, both on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2).

Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2): lower damping reduces quantization bias and exp_023's ttt_epochs=2 provides ~130s eval headroom to prevent the overrun seen in all prior damping-0.005 attempts.
Set min_lr=0.1 to floor LR at 10% of max during warmdown, preventing late-training LR collapse; training-only change with zero eval overhead, validated by PR #1787.

GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005 + calibration_batches 64→48 on top of exp_023: saves ~10s of GPTQ calibration to reliably clear environment-jitter eval overrun (exp_039 overran by only 4.6s with same ttt_epochs=2), while lower damping improves quantization quality.

Add zero-initialized per-block value residual (v_resid_alpha scalar) that lerps a V-skip into the attention output after XSA, providing a direct gradient path through c_v and an identity shortcut that improves data-efficiency in the undertrained regime.
min_lr=0.1 (LR floor at 10% of max) + gptq_calibration_batches 64→40 to create reliable eval headroom; min_lr improves late training, calib reduction saves ~12-15s eval.

ttt_epochs 2→1 + GPTQ damping 0.005 + calibration_batches 48 on exp_023: TTT dominates eval time (exp_039/046 both killed at exactly 604.6s regardless of calibration changes), so cutting another TTT epoch (saving ~130s more) is the only reliable fix; 3→2 improved quality in exp_023, 2→1 may continue that trend.
min_lr=0.1 + ttt_epochs 2→1 to clear eval budget: saves ~29s on slow environments (targeting ~575s max eval), allowing min_lr training improvement to be measured cleanly.
SWA during warmdown (equal-weight snapshots every 0.72% of budget, ~100 snapshots) replaces EMA at end of training, giving a flatter minimum; plus halved GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005).

Disable SNOO (snoo_k=0), reduce GPTQ calibration batches 64→40 to clear eval overrun, lower GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, and split Muon WD (MLP=0.115 / attn=0.095) to replicate exp_006's training quality while definitively clearing the eval budget.

Increase eval_stride 64→96 to cut SW-eval + TTT-scoring windows by 33%, clearing the 4.4s eval overrun seen in exp_026, while keeping GPTQ damping 0.005, split Muon WD (MLP=0.115/attn=0.095), calib batches 40, and SNOO disabled.

Eliminate 2 torch.compile calls in eval: (1) skip compiled_model=torch.compile(eval_model) for quantized eval, (2) skip torch._dynamo.reset() before TTT and reuse eval_model so eval_val_ttt's internal torch.compile gets a cache hit from eval_val_sliding — saving ~200s of compilation overhead to clear the persistent 5s eval overrun.

Skip torch._dynamo.reset() before TTT and reuse eval_model (instead of re-deserializing ttt_model) so eval_val_ttt's torch.compile(forward_logits) gets a cache hit from eval_val_sliding's compile, saving ~100s of TTT recompilation to clear the 5s eval budget overrun.

Remove torch.compile inside eval_val_ttt scoring loop (use base_model.forward_logits directly instead of compiled version), saving ~100s of TTT recompilation while keeping correct _dynamo.reset()+deserialize flow for a clean TTT model state.

Skip pre-quantization eval (torch._dynamo.reset + timed_eval pre-quantization post-ema) to eliminate one torch.compile call (~68s compile + ~17s inference = ~85s savings), clearing the persistent 5s eval budget overrun.

Skip eval_val_sliding (quantized_sliding_window metric) entirely — primary metric is quantized_ttt which does its own sliding-window scoring; this saves ~118s (compile #3 ~68s + SW inference ~50s), bringing eval from 605s to ~487s. Added back torch._dynamo.reset() before GPTQ to fix the crash seen in exp_065.

FreqGPTQ interleaved (no pre-collection overhead, token freq estimated from first 8 calibration batches with sqrt(2) boost for top-100 tokens) + GPTQ damping 0.005 (halved from 0.01) + TTT freeze first 2 blocks during adaptation

Stack label smoothing (ε=0.1), split Muon WD (MLP=0.115 vs attn=0.095), GPTQ damping 0.005, freq-weighted GPTQ (sqrt2 top-100), and reduce calib batches 64→48 to escape eval budget overrun while improving training regularization and quantization quality.

Label smoothing 0.1 in training cross-entropy: zero computational overhead (unlike z-loss which added logsumexp to every TTT backward pass, causing eval overrun), regularizes overconfidence in the undertrained 10-min model.

Revert TTT optimizer from non-fused AdamW back to SGD (lr=0.005, momentum=0.9) + keep reduced eval overhead (48 calib batches, 3 TTT epochs) + add GPTQ Hessian damping 0.005 (vs 0.01) for tighter quantization.
SWA snapshots in last 10% of training only (frac≥0.90, ~14 snapshots at low LR near convergence) + lower GPTQ damping (0.005) + calibration_batches 64→56 to reclaim ~4s eval headroom.

Reduce ttt_epochs 3→2 to fix systematic eval budget overrun: this environment runs ~605s for the 3-epoch baseline eval, all prior experiments overran. 2 epochs saves ~130s of TTT, bringing eval_s to ~475s. Expected val_bpb ~1.074-1.078, still a keep vs reference 1.081.

Skip redundant eval_val_sliding when TTT is enabled (saves ~240s since TTT already scores all windows), with full quality settings: 4 TTT epochs, 64 calib batches, GPTQ damping 0.005, SGD TTT lr=0.005.
Skip eval_val_sliding when TTT enabled (saves ~61s) + keep 3 TTT epochs — TTT dominates eval (~500s for 3 epochs), so 3 epochs + no redundant sliding eval gives ~540s total eval, within budget.
Skip second deserialize() before TTT by reusing eval_model directly (saves ~5-10s deserialization overhead) while keeping sliding_window eval for GPU kernel warmup that TTT needs.

Fix TTT backward crash from exp_059: clear RoPE cos/sin cache (inference_mode tensors from sliding_window eval break autograd version tracking) before reusing eval_model for TTT — preserves the 248s eval speedup from model reuse.

Skip torch._dynamo.reset() before TTT — eval_val_sliding already compiled eval_model.forward_logits, so TTT's torch.compile is a cache hit (~0s) instead of fresh compile (~240s), saving the full eval budget.

Double ttt_chunk_tokens (32768→65536) to cut TTT backward passes 4×, reducing TTT training time from ~370s to ~100s and bringing eval within 600s budget; combined with GPTQ damping 0.005 (was 0.01) and SGD TTT optimizer.

Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 (less quantization bias) and add split MLP/attention weight decay in Muon (muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for MLP matrices, muon_wd=0.095 for attention matrices) as described in INIT.md SOTA stack.
FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian toward top-100 most frequent tokens (sqrt(2) activation multiplier → 2x Hessian weight) to reduce quantization error on tokens that dominate BPB.
FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian calibration toward top-100 most frequent tokens (sqrt(2) activation boost) so quantization minimizes error on the tokens that dominate BPB.
Same as exp_006 (GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, split MLP/attn Muon WD 0.115/0.095) but reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→60 to clear the 3.9s eval budget overrun.
FreqGPTQ (freq_batches=4): bias GPTQ Hessian toward top-100 most frequent tokens via sqrt(2) activation multiplier, with reduced phase-1 scan (4 batches) to avoid eval budget overrun.
FreqGPTQ (top-100 token sqrt(2) Hessian boost) + lower GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005) + larger TTT batch_seqs (32→48) to stay within eval budget.
GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 only (reverted split MLP WD which caused systematic +4s eval overhead); calibration_batches=60 for eval budget margin.

FreqGPTQ optimized: precompute per-batch frequency weights once (not per-hook), in-place multiply to avoid extra 200MB tensor allocation — eliminates ~35x redundant gathers that caused prior 4s eval overrun.

FreqGPTQ with pre-computed per-batch frequency weights (fixes 2816 hook-internal gather ops → single gather per batch), damping=0.005, batch_seqs reverted to 32; should fit eval budget and improve val_bpb via Hessian bias toward top-100 frequent tokens.
GPTQ damping 0.005 + ttt_epochs 3→2 / ttt_lr 0.005→0.007: reduce eval budget by ~60s (TTT backward passes) to clear the systematic 4s overrun from damping=0.005 brotli compression difference.
Lower GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005) for more precise Hessian-guided quantization, plus earlier loop activation (0.35→0.20) so recurrent layers train at peak LR for 8% of budget before warmdown begins.

Split MLP vs attention Muon weight decay (muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for MLP, muon_wd=0.095 for attn) with all other params at baseline — pure training change, no GPTQ or TTT modifications, to avoid the systematic 4s eval overrun caused by GPTQ damping=0.005.

Lower GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005 for more precise Hessian-compensated quantization, move enable_looping_at 0.35→0.20 so depth-recurrence activates during peak-LR phase, and reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→48 to save ~5-6s eval time and stay under the 600s budget.

Fix RoPE inference-tensor crash in TTT adaptation by clearing _cos_cached/_sin_cached on eval_model before eval_val_sliding compiles forward_logits; this ensures the compiled graph uses regular no_grad tensors (not inference_mode tensors), enabling TTT backward to succeed. Preserves ~78s eval savings from removing dynamo.reset+re-deserialize before TTT (exp_081 confirmed eval_s=361s before crash).

Remove torch._dynamo.reset() before pre-quantization eval so training-phase compiled graph stays cached and avoids ~68s recompile, bringing eval_s from ~604s into budget; also retains MLP-specific Muon WD split (muon_wd_mlp=0.115) from prior attempt.
Remove pre-quant eval + all dynamo.reset() before eval (matching exp_072's proven ~120s savings) combined with min_lr=0.1 quality improvement, targeting val_bpb < 1.078936.

Explicitly del base_model+compiled_model then empty_cache after serialize to free training GPU memory before loading eval model, enabling no-dynamo-reset eval start (~120s savings) without OOM crash, plus min_lr=0.1 quality improvement.

Value residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): blend block-0 c_v output into V of blocks 1-10 via zero-init learnable vr_alpha; add LOWBIT_LAYERS support defaulting to int5 for blocks.10.attn.c_k to recover ~10-15 KB brotli headroom lost in exp_177.
